title: Siemens U273 B11/S7 detector parametric threshold sweep
status: S7 17-19/17-18 contact function is encoded; diode curves and active B11 loading remain parametric
warning: C11 is modeled here as an equivalent storage capacitor with a swept release load. This is not yet the full B11 transistor regulator.

s7ContactTable.note: User-confirmed drawing state: S7 common node 17 is switched to 18. D1 ZL10 remains in the limiter detector path.
Opposite switch state inferred for compressor mode: S7 common node 17 is switched to 19, bypassing the ZL10 contribution.

detectorScenarios.name: limiter_low_drop
detectorScenarios.note: Limiter bound with ZL10 active and low diode/rectifier drops.

detectorScenarios.name: limiter_nominal
detectorScenarios.note: Nominal limiter hypothesis: ZL10 active, SSD55 plus rectifier drops chosen around the printed 8 V RMS threshold.

detectorScenarios.name: limiter_high_drop
detectorScenarios.note: Limiter bound with ZL10 active and high diode/rectifier drops.

detectorScenarios.name: limiter_zener_minus10pct
detectorScenarios.note: Sensitivity bound: ZL10 effective voltage 10 percent lower, nominal diode/rectifier drops.

detectorScenarios.name: limiter_zener_plus10pct
detectorScenarios.note: Sensitivity bound: ZL10 effective voltage 10 percent higher, nominal diode/rectifier drops.

detectorScenarios.name: compressor_partial_zener_bound
detectorScenarios.note: Conservative bound kept for sensitivity: S7 effectively leaves half of the ZL10 threshold in the path.

detectorScenarios.name: compressor_s7_17_to_19_zener_bypassed
detectorScenarios.note: Compressor hypothesis tied to the opposite S7 state: 17-19 bypasses the ZL10 contribution.
